# UC-17.4.2 · Cloudflare Bot Management Score Distribution and Challenge Pass Rate (`cloudflare:json`)

## Description

Bot scores quantify automated abuse versus humans. A collapsing median score or falling challenge pass rates indicates credential stuffing or scraping.

## Value

Protects login and checkout flows while avoiding over-blocking legitimate automation partners.

## Implementation

Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.

## SPL

```spl
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| spath BotScore
| spath BotScoreSrc
| bin BotScore span=10
| stats count by BotScore
| eventstats sum(count) as tot
| eval pct=round(100*count/tot,2)
| sort BotScore
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Histogram (BotScore), line chart (median score), single value (automated request %).

## References

- [Cloudflare Logpush documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/)
- [Splunkbase — Cloudflare App for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4501)
